comments.body:
Uh-oh...the RHEL-7 test doesn't seem very stable.  It appears to flip between 100% pass and 50% fail: https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5761991525269504  @baude this looks like the thing we were scratching our heads over (on IRC) - where many tests fail with 'permission-denied'.

I think I want to do a few more re-runs here before we merge.

/hold

This is what I see while the tests are running & failing:

```
[root@cirrus-task-5174087543422976 ~]# tailf /var/log/audit/audit.log  | grep -i avc
type=AVC msg=audit(1548353391.953:2632): avc:  denied  { setattr } for  pid=8164 comm="runc:[2:INIT]" name="" dev="pipefs" ino=187893 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_runtime_t:s0 tcontext=system_u:system_r:unconfined_service_t:s0 tclass=fifo_file permissive=0
type=AVC msg=audit(1548353394.661:2653): avc:  denied  { setattr } for  pid=8356 comm="runc:[2:INIT]" name="" dev="pipefs" ino=189552 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_runtime_t:s0 tcontext=system_u:system_r:unconfined_service_t:s0 tclass=fifo_file permissive=0
type=AVC msg=audit(1548353396.110:2674): avc:  denied  { setattr } for  pid=8540 comm="runc:[2:INIT]" name="" dev="pipefs" ino=189784 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_runtime_t:s0 tcontext=system_u:system_r:unconfined_service_t:s0 tclass=fifo_file permissive=0
type=AVC msg=audit(1548353398.834:2695): avc:  denied  { setattr } for  pid=8724 comm="runc:[2:INIT]" name="" dev="pipefs" ino=190182 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_runtime_t:s0 tcontext=system_u:system_r:unconfined_service_t:s0 tclass=fifo_file permissive=0
type=AVC msg=audit(1548353402.374:2716): avc:  denied  { setattr } for  pid=8977 comm="runc:[2:INIT]" name="" dev="pipefs" ino=192892 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_runtime_t:s0 tcontext=system_u:system_r:unconfined_service_t:s0 tclass=fifo_file permissive=0
type=AVC msg=audit(1548353405.142:2737): avc:  denied  { setattr } for  pid=9161 comm="runc:[2:INIT]" name="" dev="pipefs" ino=192434 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_runtime_t:s0 tcontext=system_u:system_r:unconfined_service_t:s0 tclass=fifo_file permissive=0
type=AVC msg=audit(1548353406.637:2758): avc:  denied  { setattr } for  pid=9345 comm="runc:[2:INIT]" name="" dev="pipefs" ino=194971 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_runtime_t:s0 tcontext=system_u:system_r:unconfined_service_t:s0 tclass=fifo_file permissive=0
```

@rhatdan I grabbed the VM while the e2e tests are running and see the above audit messages.

I tried running a ``fixfiles restore`` but keep seeing those same AVC's.  Otherwise I believe the only things that should change from one run to the next is the podman build and the clock.  Meaning it's the same VM image with same packages (no test-time updates).  It should only be building/installing podman (from this PR) and executing tests.  So it's kind of insane that we should have inconsistent results.  Under `/tmp` I see:

```
...snip...
drwx------. root root system_u:object_r:tmp_t:s0       crio
drwx------. root root system_u:object_r:tmp_t:s0       crio-run
-rw-r--r--. root root system_u:object_r:tmp_t:s0       fedora-minimal-latest.tar
drwxrwxrwt. root root system_u:object_r:user_fonts_t:s0 .font-unix
drwx------. root root system_u:object_r:tmp_t:s0       ginkgo503937553
drwxrwxrwt. root root system_u:object_r:user_tmp_t:s0  .ICE-unix
-rw-------. root root system_u:object_r:tmp_t:s0       integration_test738108251
-rw-r--r--. root root system_u:object_r:tmp_t:s0       os-release.test
-rw-r--r--. root root system_u:object_r:tmp_t:s0       packer_sha256sums
-rw-r--r--. root root system_u:object_r:tmp_t:s0       pause-3.1.tar
drwx------. root root system_u:object_r:tmp_t:s0       podman-test013707090
drwx------. root root system_u:object_r:tmp_t:s0       podman-test077555123
...snip...
drwx------. root root system_u:object_r:tmp_t:s0       systemd-private-cfaedc8716cc429db1b98365b39a6c31-chronyd.service-AzGy4F
```

Any idea what could cause this pass/fail change between runs given mostly all the same inputs?
Looks like your daemon cri-o is running as unconfined_service_t rather then container_runtime_t. Usually caused by the crio executable not being labeled container_runtime_exec_t.

From IRC:

```
<cevich> dwalsh, that's exactly it.  I just did chcon -t container_runtime_exec_t $GOSRC/bin/podman and it fixed it
<cevich> dwalsh, so strange how this could change between test-runs though :S
<cevich> dwalsh, I needa go look at what crio is labeled too?
```

@rhatdan PTAL that last ``Set SELinux type on bin/podman after install`` commit.  That way seems more developer and automation friendly.  Though I can put it into the test scripts as well if that's better for some reason.  I still have no idea why/how this could have been passing/failing from one run to the next or between RHEL/Fedora.  Let me know if that's important and I'll dig deeper.
@mheon @baude PTAL, this turns on RHEL 7 testing :smile:
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2206) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
